= Bash Utilities Specification
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// bust_:  Tabtarget (launcher naming vesture)
// busd:   Dispatch (non-terminal, tabtarget system umbrella)
// busdc_: Dispatch Colophon (routing identifier)
// busdf_: Dispatch Frontispiece (human-readable description)
// busdi_: Dispatch Imprint (embedded parameter/target specifier)
// busdm_: Dispatch Formulary (routing component)
// busdl_: Dispatch Launcher (bootstrap script)
// bukro_: Kit Regime Operation (kindle/validate/render)
// burc_:  Configuration Regime (project-level settings)
// burs_:  Station Regime (developer-level settings)
// burd_:  Dispatch Runtime (ephemeral state assembled during dispatch)
// bure_:  Environment Regime (ambient caller-supplied behavioral overrides)
// busi:   Invocation (test invocation umbrella)
// busit_: Invocation Tier (tier classification)
// busig_: Invocation Global (capture state set by zbuto_invoke)
// busx_:  Case Boundary (test case isolation runner)
// busff_: Fact-File (inter-step communication via output files)
// buf_:   Fact Module (dual-write fact-file primitives)
// burx_:  Exchange Regime (cross-project dispatch state)
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

// Tabtarget Vesture
:bust_tabtarget:        <<bust_tabtarget,Tabtarget Vesture>>

// Dispatch
:busdc_colophon:        <<busdc_colophon,Colophon>>
:busdc_colophon_s:      <<busdc_colophon,Colophons>>
:busdf_frontispiece:    <<busdf_frontispiece,Frontispiece>>
:busdf_frontispiece_s:  <<busdf_frontispiece,Frontispieces>>
:busdi_imprint:         <<busdi_imprint,Imprint>>
:busdi_imprint_s:       <<busdi_imprint,Imprints>>
:busdm_formulary:       <<busdm_formulary,Formulary>>
:busdm_formulary_s:     <<busdm_formulary,Formularies>>
:busdl_launcher:        <<busdl_launcher,Launcher>>
:busdl_launcher_s:      <<busdl_launcher,Launchers>>

// Axial Regime Terms (from AXLA — matching RBSA)
:axrg_assignment:                  <<axrg_assignment,Assignment>>
:axrg_assignment_s:                <<axrg_assignment,Assignments>>
:axrg_prefix:                      <<axrg_prefix,Regime Prefix>>
:axrg_regime:                      <<axrg_regime,Regime>>
:axrg_regime_s:                    <<axrg_regime,Regimes>>
:axrg_variable:                    <<axrg_variable,Regime Variable>>
:axrg_variable_s:                  <<axrg_variable,Regime Variables>>

// Axial Format Terms (from AXLA — matching RBSA)
:axf_bash:                         <<axf_bash,Bash Format>>

// BUK Regime Operation Terms
:bukro_kindle:                     <<bukro_kindle,BUK Kindle>>
:bukro_validate:                   <<bukro_validate,BUK Validate>>
:bukro_render:                     <<bukro_render,BUK Render>>

// BUK Regime Prefixes
:burc_prefix:                      <<burc_prefix,BURC_>>
:burh_prefix:                      <<burh_prefix,BURH_>>
:burs_prefix:                      <<burs_prefix,BURS_>>

// BURC Regime: Configuration (project-level BUK settings)
:burc_regime:                      <<burc_regime,BURC Regime>>
:burc_station_file:                <<burc_station_file,BURC_STATION_FILE>>
:burc_tabtarget_dir:               <<burc_tabtarget_dir,BURC_TABTARGET_DIR>>
:burc_tabtarget_delimiter:         <<burc_tabtarget_delimiter,BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER>>
:burc_tools_dir:                   <<burc_tools_dir,BURC_TOOLS_DIR>>
:burc_project_root:                <<burc_project_root,BURC_PROJECT_ROOT>>
:burc_managed_kits:                <<burc_managed_kits,BURC_MANAGED_KITS>>
:burc_temp_root_dir:               <<burc_temp_root_dir,BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR>>
:burc_output_root_dir:             <<burc_output_root_dir,BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR>>
:burc_log_last:                    <<burc_log_last,BURC_LOG_LAST>>
:burc_log_ext:                     <<burc_log_ext,BURC_LOG_EXT>>

// BURH Regime: Host (per-user SSH connection profiles)
:burh_regime:                      <<burh_regime,BURH Regime>>
:burh_host:                        <<burh_host,BURH_HOST>>
:burh_user:                        <<burh_user,BURH_USER>>
:burh_alias:                       <<burh_alias,BURH_ALIAS>>
:burh_ssh_pubkey:                  <<burh_ssh_pubkey,BURH_SSH_PUBKEY>>
:burh_key_file:                    <<burh_key_file,BURH_KEY_FILE>>
:burh_command:                     <<burh_command,BURH_COMMAND>>

// BURS Regime: Station (developer-level BUK settings)
:burs_regime:                      <<burs_regime,BURS Regime>>
:burs_user:                        <<burs_user,BURS_USER>>
:burs_log_dir:                     <<burs_log_dir,BURS_LOG_DIR>>

// BURD Regime: Dispatch Runtime (ephemeral dispatch state)
:burd_regime:                      <<burd_regime,BURD Regime>>
:burd_prefix:                      <<burd_prefix,BURD_>>

// BURD Groups
:burd_group_input:                 <<burd_group_input,Input Variables>>
:burd_group_computed:              <<burd_group_computed,Computed Variables>>
:burd_group_parsed:                <<burd_group_parsed,Parsed Variables>>

// BURD Input Variables
:burd_regime_file:                 <<burd_regime_file,BURD_REGIME_FILE>>
:burd_no_log:                      <<burd_no_log,BURD_NO_LOG>>
:burd_interactive:                 <<burd_interactive,BURD_INTERACTIVE>>
:burd_coordinator_script:          <<burd_coordinator_script,BURD_COORDINATOR_SCRIPT>>
:burd_launcher:                    <<burd_launcher,BURD_LAUNCHER>>
:burd_station_file:                <<burd_station_file,BURD_STATION_FILE>>
:burd_term_cols:                   <<burd_term_cols,BURD_TERM_COLS>>

// BURD Computed Variables
:burd_now_stamp:                   <<burd_now_stamp,BURD_NOW_STAMP>>
:burd_now_epoch:                   <<burd_now_epoch,BURD_NOW_EPOCH>>
:burd_temp_dir:                    <<burd_temp_dir,BURD_TEMP_DIR>>
:burd_output_dir:                  <<burd_output_dir,BURD_OUTPUT_DIR>>
:burd_transcript:                  <<burd_transcript,BURD_TRANSCRIPT>>
:burd_git_context:                 <<burd_git_context,BURD_GIT_CONTEXT>>
:burd_log_last:                    <<burd_log_last,BURD_LOG_LAST>>
:burd_log_same:                    <<burd_log_same,BURD_LOG_SAME>>
:burd_log_hist:                    <<burd_log_hist,BURD_LOG_HIST>>

// BURD Parsed Variables
:burd_command:                     <<burd_command,BURD_COMMAND>>
:burd_target:                      <<burd_target,BURD_TARGET>>
:burd_cli_args:                    <<burd_cli_args,BURD_CLI_ARGS>>
:burd_token_1:                     <<burd_token_1,BURD_TOKEN_1>>
:burd_token_2:                     <<burd_token_2,BURD_TOKEN_2>>
:burd_token_3:                     <<burd_token_3,BURD_TOKEN_3>>
:burd_token_4:                     <<burd_token_4,BURD_TOKEN_4>>
:burd_token_5:                     <<burd_token_5,BURD_TOKEN_5>>

// BURE Regime: Environment (ambient caller-supplied behavioral overrides)
:bure_prefix:                      <<bure_prefix,BURE_>>
:bure_regime:                      <<bure_regime,BURE Regime>>
:bure_countdown:                   <<bure_countdown,BURE_COUNTDOWN>>
:bure_verbose:                     <<bure_verbose,BURE_VERBOSE>>
:bure_color:                       <<bure_color,BURE_COLOR>>
:bure_tweak_name:                  <<bure_tweak_name,BURE_TWEAK_NAME>>
:bure_tweak_value:                 <<bure_tweak_value,BURE_TWEAK_VALUE>>
:bure_label:                       <<bure_label,BURE_LABEL>>

// Invocation Tier
:busit_unit:                      <<busit_unit,Unit Tier>>
:busit_tabtarget:                 <<busit_tabtarget,Tabtarget Tier>>
:busit_launch:                    <<busit_launch,Launch Tier>>

// Invocation Globals
:busig_stdout:                    <<busig_stdout,ZBUTO_STDOUT>>
:busig_stderr:                    <<busig_stderr,ZBUTO_STDERR>>
:busig_status:                    <<busig_status,ZBUTO_STATUS>>
:busig_burv_output:               <<busig_burv_output,ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT>>

// Case Boundary
:busx_tcase:                      <<busx_tcase,Case Boundary>>
:busx_temp_dir:                   <<busx_temp_dir,BUT_TEMP_DIR>>
:busx_burv_root:                  <<busx_burv_root,BUTE_BURV_ROOT>>

// Fact-File
:busff_fact_file:                 <<busff_fact_file,Fact-File>>

// Fact Module
:buf_write_fact:                  <<buf_write_fact,buf_write_fact>>
:buf_burx_env:                    <<buf_burx_env,BUF_burx_env>>

// BURX Regime: Exchange (cross-project dispatch state)
:burx_prefix:                     <<burx_prefix,BURX_>>
:burx_regime:                     <<burx_regime,BURX Regime>>
:burx_pid:                        <<burx_pid,BURX_PID>>
:burx_began_at:                   <<burx_began_at,BURX_BEGAN_AT>>
:burx_tabtarget:                  <<burx_tabtarget,BURX_TABTARGET>>
:burx_temp_dir:                   <<burx_temp_dir,BURX_TEMP_DIR>>
:burx_transcript:                 <<burx_transcript,BURX_TRANSCRIPT>>
:burx_log_hist:                   <<burx_log_hist,BURX_LOG_HIST>>
:burx_label:                      <<burx_label,BURX_LABEL>>
:burx_exit_status:                <<burx_exit_status,BURX_EXIT_STATUS>>
:burx_ended_at:                   <<burx_ended_at,BURX_ENDED_AT>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.

BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.

For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.

== Tabtarget Vesture

[[bust_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{bust_tabtarget}::
Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
+
* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
* separator: `.`

content.content:
200	* separator: `.`
201	* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
202	* envelope: `.sh`
203	+
204	Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
205	+
206	Examples:
207	+
208	* `rbw-cr.Rack.sh` — RBW workbench, Rack operation
209	* `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
210	* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
211	* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation
212	
213	== Dispatch
214	
215	The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
216	A
217	{busdl_launcher}
218	validates arguments and delegates to a
219	{busdm_formulary}.
220	The
221	{busdc_colophon}
222	identifies the routing path;
223	the
224	{busdf_frontispiece}
225	provides human-readable description;
226	the
227	{busdi_imprint}
228	embeds target parameters.
229	
230	[[busdc_colophon]]
231	//axl_voices axt_string
232	{busdc_colophon}::
233	The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
234	A
235	{busdc_colophon}
236	determines which
237	{busdm_formulary}
238	handles the dispatch.
239	Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
240	+
241	The
242	{busdc_colophon}
243	is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
244	+
245	Examples:
246	+
247	* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
248	* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
249	* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench
250	
251	[[busdf_frontispiece]]
252	//axl_voices axt_string
253	{busdf_frontispiece}::
254	The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
255	A
256	{busdf_frontispiece}
257	uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
258	+
259	Appears after the
260	{busdc_colophon}
261	and dot separator.
262	+
263	Examples: `Build`, `Rack`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.
264	
265	[[busdi_imprint]]
266	//axl_voices axt_string
267	{busdi_imprint}::
268	An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
269	An
270	{busdi_imprint}
271	specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
272	+
273	Appears after a second dot separator following the
274	{busdf_frontispiece}.
275	+
276	Examples:
277	+
278	* `tadmor` in `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
279	* `srjcl` in `rbw-cr.Rack.srjcl.sh`
280	
281	[[busdm_formulary]]
282	//axl_voices axo_entity
283	{busdm_formulary}::
284	A component that routes
285	{busdc_colophon_s}
286	to implementations.
287	Each
288	{busdm_formulary}
289	registers handlers for a set of
290	{busdc_colophon_s}
291	and dispatches to the appropriate function.
292	+
293	Types:
294	+
295	* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
296	* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
297	+
298	A
299	{busdm_formulary}
300	is sourced by the
301	{busdl_launcher}
302	and provides the dispatch function.
303	
304	[[busdl_launcher]]
305	//axl_voices axo_entity
306	{busdl_launcher}::
307	A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
308	{busdm_formulary}.
309	A
310	{busdl_launcher}
311	is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
312	+
313	Naming follows
314	{bust_tabtarget}:
315	`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
316	+
317	Responsibilities:
318	+
319	* Parse
320	{busdc_colophon}
321	from own filename
322	* Validate required arguments
323	* Source the target
324	{busdm_formulary}
325	* Invoke the dispatched function
326	
327	== Routing Behavior
328	
329	=== Dispatch Flow
330	
331	. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
332	. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
333	* {busdc_colophon}:
334	`rbw-cr`
335	* {busdf_frontispiece}:
336	`Rack`
337	* {busdi_imprint}:
338	`tadmor`
339	. Launcher sources the appropriate
340	{busdm_formulary}
341	(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
342	. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
343	. Target function executes with imprint as parameter
344	
345	=== Workbench Pattern
346	
347	[source,bash]
348	----
349	# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh
350	
351	{prefix}w_dispatch() {
352	    local frontispiece="$1"
353	    local imprint="$2"
354	    shift 2
355	
356	    case "$frontispiece" in
357	        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
358	        Rack)           {prefix}w_rack "$imprint" "$@" ;;
359	        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
360	    esac
361	}
362	----
363	
364	=== Testbench Pattern
365	
366	Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:
367	
368	[source,bash]
369	----
370	# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh
371	
372	{prefix}t_dispatch() {
373	    local frontispiece="$1"
374	    shift
375	
376	    case "$frontispiece" in
377	        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
378	        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
379	        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
380	    esac
381	}
382	----
383	
384	=== Test Registry Model
385	
386	The test registry (`butr_registry.sh`) uses two orthogonal enrollment dimensions:
387	
388	**Fixture** — init/setup execution context.
389	Each test case belongs to exactly one fixture.
390	Fixtures provide isolation via `_tsuite` subshell boundaries.
391	Registered with `butr_fixture_enroll(name, init_fn, setup_fn)`.
392	
393	**Suite** — cross-cutting selection group (N:M with cases).
394	A case can belong to multiple suites.
395	Suites classify by dependency tier (what environment must exist), not by test methodology.
396	A suite is a membership tag.
397	Suites enable suite dispatch: run a cross-cutting subset of cases grouped by their owning fixtures.
398	Suite context is set with `butr_suite_enroll(SUITE1 [SUITE2...])` before case enrollments.
399	Queried by `butd_run_suite(suite_name)`.

file.content:
* separator: `.`
* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
* envelope: `.sh`
+
Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-cr.Rack.sh` — RBW workbench, Rack operation
* `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation

== Dispatch

The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
A
{busdl_launcher}
validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
The
{busdc_colophon}
identifies the routing path;
the
{busdf_frontispiece}
provides human-readable description;
the
{busdi_imprint}
embeds target parameters.

[[busdc_colophon]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdc_colophon}::
The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdc_colophon}
determines which
{busdm_formulary}
handles the dispatch.
Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
+
The
{busdc_colophon}
is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench

[[busdf_frontispiece]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdf_frontispiece}::
The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdf_frontispiece}
uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
+
Appears after the
{busdc_colophon}
and dot separator.
+
Examples: `Build`, `Rack`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.

[[busdi_imprint]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdi_imprint}::
An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
An
{busdi_imprint}
specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
+
Appears after a second dot separator following the
{busdf_frontispiece}.
+
Examples:
+
* `tadmor` in `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
* `srjcl` in `rbw-cr.Rack.srjcl.sh`

[[busdm_formulary]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busdm_formulary}::
A component that routes
{busdc_colophon_s}
to implementations.
Each
{busdm_formulary}
registers handlers for a set of
{busdc_colophon_s}
and dispatches to the appropriate function.
+
Types:
+
* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
+
A
{busdm_formulary}
is sourced by the
{busdl_launcher}
and provides the dispatch function.

[[busdl_launcher]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busdl_launcher}::
A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
A
{busdl_launcher}
is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
+
Naming follows
{bust_tabtarget}:
`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Responsibilities:
+
* Parse
{busdc_colophon}
from own filename
* Validate required arguments
* Source the target
{busdm_formulary}
* Invoke the dispatched function

== Routing Behavior

=== Dispatch Flow

. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
* {busdc_colophon}:
`rbw-cr`
* {busdf_frontispiece}:
`Rack`
* {busdi_imprint}:
`tadmor`
. Launcher sources the appropriate
{busdm_formulary}
(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
. Target function executes with imprint as parameter

=== Workbench Pattern

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh

{prefix}w_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    local imprint="$2"
    shift 2

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        Rack)           {prefix}w_rack "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Testbench Pattern

Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh

{prefix}t_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    shift

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Test Registry Model

The test registry (`butr_registry.sh`) uses two orthogonal enrollment dimensions:

**Fixture** — init/setup execution context.
Each test case belongs to exactly one fixture.
Fixtures provide isolation via `_tsuite` subshell boundaries.
Registered with `butr_fixture_enroll(name, init_fn, setup_fn)`.

**Suite** — cross-cutting selection group (N:M with cases).
A case can belong to multiple suites.
Suites classify by dependency tier (what environment must exist), not by test methodology.
A suite is a membership tag.
Suites enable suite dispatch: run a cross-cutting subset of cases grouped by their owning fixtures.
Suite context is set with `butr_suite_enroll(SUITE1 [SUITE2...])` before case enrollments.
Queried by `butd_run_suite(suite_name)`.

content.content:
400	
401	Standard suite constants (defined as `readonly` in testbench kindle):
402	
403	* `BUTR_SUITE_FAST` — no external dependencies, CI-safe
404	* `BUTR_SUITE_SERVICE` — needs credentials, no container runtime
405	* `BUTR_SUITE_COMPLETE` — full test matrix including container runtime
406	
407	Dispatch entry points:
408	
409	* `butd_run_all()` — all fixtures, all cases
410	* `butd_run_fixture(name [case...])` — single fixture, optional case filter
411	* `butd_run_one(fn)` — single case with its fixture setup
412	* `butd_run_suite(suite)` — cross-cutting suite, grouped by fixture
413	
414	=== Colophon Conventions
415	
416	{busdc_colophon_s}
417	follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:
418	
419	* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
420	* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
421	* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-cr-` for crucible rack operations)
422	
423	The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.
424	
425	== Test Invocation
426	
427	The test invocation vocabulary defines how test cases exercise code under controlled capture.
428	All invocation tiers share a common primitive,
429	`zbuto_invoke`,
430	which captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and optional BURV output into
431	{busig_stdout},
432	{busig_stderr},
433	{busig_status},
434	and
435	{busig_burv_output}.
436	
437	=== Invocation Tiers
438	
439	Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:
440	
441	[[busit_unit]]
442	//axl_voices axo_entity
443	{busit_unit}::
444	Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
445	No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
446	+
447	API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`
448	
449	[[busit_tabtarget]]
450	//axl_voices axo_entity
451	{busit_tabtarget}::
452	Resolves a
453	{busdc_colophon}
454	to a
455	{busdl_launcher}
456	file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
457	Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
458	+
459	API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`
460	
461	[[busit_launch]]
462	//axl_voices axo_entity
463	{busit_launch}::
464	Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
465	{busdl_launcher}
466	file.
467	Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
468	+
469	API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`
470	
471	=== Capture Globals
472	
473	Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.
474	
475	[[busig_stdout]]
476	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
477	{busig_stdout}::
478	Captured stdout from the invoked command.
479	
480	[[busig_stderr]]
481	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
482	{busig_stderr}::
483	Captured stderr from the invoked command.
484	
485	[[busig_status]]
486	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
487	{busig_status}::
488	Exit code from the invoked command.
489	
490	[[busig_burv_output]]
491	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
492	{busig_burv_output}::
493	BURV output directory for the invocation.
494	Empty when
495	{busx_burv_root}
496	is not set.
497	When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
498	`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.
499	
500	=== Case Boundary
501	
502	[[busx_tcase]]
503	//axl_voices axo_entity
504	{busx_tcase}::
505	The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
506	Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
507	Sets
508	{busx_temp_dir}
509	and
510	{busx_burv_root}
511	before invoking the case function.
512	+
513	The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.
514	
515	[[busx_temp_dir]]
516	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
517	{busx_temp_dir}::
518	Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
519	{busx_tcase}.
520	Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.
521	
522	[[busx_burv_root]]
523	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
524	{busx_burv_root}::
525	Per-case BURV isolation root (`BUTE_BURV_ROOT`), set by
526	{busx_tcase}.
527	When present, all invocation tiers create per-invoke BURV directories under this root, enabling multi-step test sequences to produce and consume isolated output.
528	
529	=== Fact-File Pattern
530	
531	[[busff_fact_file]]
532	//axl_voices axo_entity
533	{busff_fact_file}::
534	Inter-step communication pattern using named output files.
535	+
536	A producer writes a value to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${CONSTANT}` during command execution.
537	A consumer reads it from `${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${CONSTANT}` after invocation.
538	The kindle constant name is the contract between producer and consumer.
539	+
540	Example:
541	+
542	[source,bash]
543	----
544	# Producer (in command implementation):
545	echo "${z_hallmark}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}"
546	
547	# Consumer (in test case, after buto_tt_expect_ok):
548	z_hallmark=$(<"${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}")
549	----
550	+
551	The `current/` path segment maps
552	{burd_output_dir}
553	inside the BURV isolation tree.
554	Fact-file constants are defined as `readonly` in kindle (e.g., `RBF_FACT_HALLMARK`).
555	
556	[[buf_write_fact]]
557	//axl_voices axo_entity
558	{buf_write_fact}::
559	Dual-write fact-file primitive.
560	Writes a named file to both
561	{burd_output_dir}
562	and
563	{burd_temp_dir}.
564	Dies if either copy already exists — a preexisting file indicates a double-write bug.
565	+
566	{burd_output_dir}
567	is the latest-command convenience copy, cleared on next dispatch.
568	{burd_temp_dir}
569	is the durable copy, addressable by anyone who captured the path.
570	+
571	All fact-file producers must use
572	{buf_write_fact}
573	to ensure durable copies exist.
574	+
575	[source,bash]
576	----
577	# Single-value fact:
578	buf_write_fact "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
579	
580	# Multi-field fact (e.g., burx.env):
581	buf_write_fact "${BUF_burx_env}" "${z_content}"
582	----
583	
584	[[buf_burx_env]]
585	//axpof_fact
586	{buf_burx_env}::
587	Tinder constant naming the
588	{burx_regime}
589	fact-file (`burx.env`).
590	Written by bud_dispatch.sh via
591	{buf_write_fact}
592	at dispatch start, with terminal fields appended at completion.
593	
594	== Regime Configuration
595	
596	BUK defines two
597	{axrg_regime_s}
598	for project and developer configuration.
599	{axrg_assignment_s}
600	use
601	{axf_bash}
602	format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).
603	
604	=== Regime Prefixes
605	
606	[[burc_prefix]]
607	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
608	{burc_prefix}::
609	Bash Utility Configuration Regime
610	
611	[[burs_prefix]]
612	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
613	{burs_prefix}::
614	Bash Utility Station Regime
615	
616	[[burh_prefix]]
617	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
618	{burh_prefix}::
619	Bash Utility Host Regime
620	
621	[[bure_prefix]]
622	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
623	{bure_prefix}::
624	Bash Utility Environment Regime
625	
626	=== Kit Regime Operations
627	
628	[[bukro_kindle]]
629	//axvr_kindle
630	{bukro_kindle}::
631	Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
632	{axrg_assignment_s}.
633	Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.
634	
635	[[bukro_validate]]
636	//axvr_validate
637	{bukro_validate}::
638	Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
639	{axrg_regime_s}.
640	Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.
641	
642	[[bukro_render]]
643	//axvr_render
644	{bukro_render}::
645	Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
646	{axrg_regime}
647	state.
648	
649	=== BURC Regime
650	
651	[[burc_regime]]
652	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
653	{burc_regime}::
654	Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
655	BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.
656	
657	[[burc_station_file]]
658	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
659	{burc_station_file}::
660	Path to the developer's personal
661	{burs_regime}
662	assignment file.
663	
664	[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
665	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
666	{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
667	Directory containing
668	{busdl_launcher}
669	scripts.
670	
671	[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
672	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
673	{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
674	Single-character token separator in
675	{bust_tabtarget}
676	inscriptions.
677	
678	[[burc_tools_dir]]
679	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
680	{burc_tools_dir}::
681	Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.
682	
683	[[burc_project_root]]
684	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
685	{burc_project_root}::
686	Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.
687	
688	[[burc_managed_kits]]
689	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
690	{burc_managed_kits}::
691	Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
692	At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
693	At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.
694	
695	[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
696	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
697	{burc_temp_root_dir}::
698	Root directory for BUK temporary files.
699	
700	[[burc_output_root_dir]]
701	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
702	{burc_output_root_dir}::
703	Root directory for BUK command output files.
704	
705	[[burc_log_last]]
706	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
707	{burc_log_last}::
708	Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.
709	
710	[[burc_log_ext]]
711	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
712	{burc_log_ext}::
713	File extension for log files, without leading dot.
714	
715	=== BURS Regime
716	
717	[[burs_regime]]
718	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
719	{burs_regime}::
720	Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
721	BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.
722	
723	[[burs_user]]
724	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
725	{burs_user}::
726	Local developer username.
727	Routes to the correct `.buk/users/` subdirectory for
728	{burh_regime}
729	profiles.
730	
731	[[burs_log_dir]]
732	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
733	{burs_log_dir}::
734	Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.
735	
736	=== BURH Regime
737	
738	[[burh_regime]]
739	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
740	{burh_regime}::
741	Per-user, per-connection-profile regime for SSH access to remote hosts.
742	Unlike singleton regimes, BURH is multi-instance: one assignment file per SSH connection,
743	stored at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/<alias>/burh.env`.
744	Each profile describes one SSH connection — one key, one alias, one entry point.
745	
746	[[burh_host]]
747	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
748	{burh_host}::
749	IP address or hostname of the remote machine.
750	
751	[[burh_user]]
752	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
753	{burh_user}::
754	Username on the remote host.
755	May differ from the local
756	{burs_user}.
757	
758	[[burh_alias]]
759	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
760	{burh_alias}::
761	SSH alias and key filename.
762	Must match the directory name containing this profile's assignment file.
763	
764	[[burh_ssh_pubkey]]
765	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
766	{burh_ssh_pubkey}::
767	Full SSH public key line (e.g., `ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host`).
768	Git-transported: no private key material.
769	Used by handbook procedures to render `authorized_keys` entries and `ssh-keygen` commands.
770	
771	[[burh_key_file]]
772	//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
773	{burh_key_file}::
774	SSH private key filename within `~/.ssh/`.
775	Empty defaults to {burh_alias} (one key per connection).
776	Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.
777	
778	[[burh_command]]
779	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
780	{burh_command}::
781	Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
782	Empty for direct-shell connections (Linux, macOS, localhost — no routing needed).
783	When set, determines which shell or environment the connection launches
784	(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).
785	
786	=== BURD Regime
787	
788	[[burd_prefix]]
789	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
790	{burd_prefix}::
791	Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime
792	
793	[[burd_regime]]
794	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
795	{burd_regime}::
796	Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
797	Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
798	The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.
799	
800	==== Input Variables
801	
802	[[burd_group_input]]
803	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
804	{burd_group_input}::
805	Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.
806	
807	[[burd_regime_file]]
808	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
809	{burd_regime_file}::
810	Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.
811	
812	[[burd_no_log]]
813	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
814	{burd_no_log}::
815	When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.
816	
817	[[burd_interactive]]
818	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
819	{burd_interactive}::
820	When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.
821	
822	[[burd_coordinator_script]]
823	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
824	{burd_coordinator_script}::
825	Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.
826	
827	[[burd_launcher]]
828	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
829	{burd_launcher}::
830	Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.
831	
832	[[burd_station_file]]
833	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
834	{burd_station_file}::
835	Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
836	Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.
837	
838	[[burd_term_cols]]
839	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
840	{burd_term_cols}::
841	Terminal column width at dispatch time.
842	Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.
843	
844	==== Computed Variables
845	
846	[[burd_group_computed]]
847	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
848	{burd_group_computed}::
849	Variables derived during dispatch setup.
850	
851	[[burd_now_stamp]]
852	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
853	{burd_now_stamp}::
854	Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.
855	
856	[[burd_now_epoch]]
857	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
858	{burd_now_epoch}::
859	UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.
860	
861	[[burd_temp_dir]]
862	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
863	{burd_temp_dir}::
864	Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.
865	
866	[[burd_output_dir]]
867	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
868	{burd_output_dir}::
869	Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.
870	
871	[[burd_transcript]]
872	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
873	{burd_transcript}::
874	Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.
875	
876	[[burd_git_context]]
877	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
878	{burd_git_context}::
879	Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.
880	
881	[[burd_log_last]]
882	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
883	{burd_log_last}::
884	Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.
885	
886	[[burd_log_same]]
887	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
888	{burd_log_same}::
889	Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.
890	
891	[[burd_log_hist]]
892	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
893	{burd_log_hist}::
894	Path to the timestamped historical log file.
895	
896	==== Parsed Variables
897	
898	[[burd_group_parsed]]
899	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
900	{burd_group_parsed}::
901	Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.
902	
903	[[burd_command]]
904	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
905	{burd_command}::
906	Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).
907	
908	[[burd_target]]
909	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
910	{burd_target}::
911	Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.
912	
913	[[burd_cli_args]]
914	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated
915	{burd_cli_args}::
916	Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.
917	
918	[[burd_token_1]]
919	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
920	{burd_token_1}::
921	Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).
922	
923	[[burd_token_2]]
924	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
925	{burd_token_2}::
926	Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).
927	
928	[[burd_token_3]]
929	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
930	{burd_token_3}::
931	Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).
932	
933	[[burd_token_4]]
934	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
935	{burd_token_4}::
936	Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.
937	
938	[[burd_token_5]]
939	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
940	{burd_token_5}::
941	Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.
942	
943	=== BURE Regime
944	
945	[[bure_regime]]
946	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
947	{bure_regime}::
948	Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
949	Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
950	Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.
951	
952	[[bure_countdown]]
953	//axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional
954	{bure_countdown}::
955	Countdown behavior override.
956	When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
957	When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
958	Any other value fails validation.
959	
960	[[bure_verbose]]
961	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
962	{bure_verbose}::
963	Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
964	0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.
965	
966	[[bure_color]]
967	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
968	{bure_color}::
969	Color output policy.
970	Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
971	Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.
972	
973	==== Tweak Mechanism
974	
975	The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
976	BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.
977	
978	Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
979	The caller sets at most one
980	{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
981	pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
982	The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.
983	
984	[[bure_tweak_name]]
985	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
986	{bure_tweak_name}::
987	Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
988	The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
989	BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
990	+
991	Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.
992	
993	[[bure_tweak_value]]
994	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
995	{bure_tweak_value}::
996	Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
997	The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
998	{bure_tweak_name}.
999	BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
1000	+
1001	Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
1002	Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.
1003	
1004	==== Exchange Labels
1005	
1006	[[bure_label]]
1007	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname axd_optional
1008	{bure_label}::
1009	Correlation label for cross-project exchange.
1010	When set, propagated verbatim into
1011	{burx_label}
1012	during dispatch.
1013	When unset, BUD writes empty string.
1014	Maximum 120 characters, xname format.
1015	+
1016	BUD writes whatever
1017	{bure_label}
1018	contains without interpreting the semantics.
1019	
1020	=== BURX Regime
1021	
1022	[[burx_prefix]]
1023	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1024	{burx_prefix}::
1025	Bash Utility Exchange Regime
1026	
1027	[[burx_regime]]
1028	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
1029	{burx_regime}::
1030	Exchange regime for cross-project dispatch state.
1031	Written by bud_dispatch.sh to both
1032	{burd_output_dir}
1033	and
1034	{burd_temp_dir}
1035	as `burx.env` via
1036	{buf_write_fact}.
1037	Unlike single-project regimes,
1038	{burx_regime}
1039	is designed to be read across a remote project boundary.
1040	+
1041	Variables appear in two phases:
1042	initial fields are written at dispatch start;
1043	terminal fields are appended at dispatch completion.
1044	Absence of
1045	{burx_exit_status}
1046	indicates the dispatch is still running.
1047	
1048	[[burx_pid]]
1049	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1050	{burx_pid}::
1051	Process ID of the dispatched command.
1052	
1053	[[burx_began_at]]
1054	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1055	{burx_began_at}::
1056	Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch started.
1057	Format: `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN`.
1058	
1059	[[burx_tabtarget]]
1060	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1061	{burx_tabtarget}::
1062	The tabtarget that was dispatched.
1063	Corresponds to
1064	{burd_target}.
1065	
1066	[[burx_temp_dir]]
1067	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1068	{burx_temp_dir}::
1069	Absolute path to the dispatch temporary directory.
1070	Corresponds to
1071	{burd_temp_dir}.
1072	
1073	[[burx_transcript]]
1074	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1075	{burx_transcript}::
1076	Absolute path to the transcript file.
1077	Corresponds to
1078	{burd_transcript}.
1079	
1080	[[burx_log_hist]]
1081	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1082	{burx_log_hist}::
1083	Absolute path to the historical log file.
1084	Corresponds to
1085	{burd_log_hist}.
1086	
1087	[[burx_label]]
1088	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1089	{burx_label}::
1090	Correlation label from
1091	{bure_label}.
1092	Always present — empty string when
1093	{bure_label}
1094	is not set.
1095	Format and interpretation are opaque to BUD.
1096	
1097	[[burx_exit_status]]
1098	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer axd_optional
1099	{burx_exit_status}::
1100	Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
1101	Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.
1102	
1103	[[burx_ended_at]]
1104	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1105	{burx_ended_at}::
1106	Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
1107	Same format as
1108	{burx_began_at}.
1109	Present only when
1110	{burx_exit_status}
1111	is present.
1112	
1113	include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

file.content:
Standard suite constants (defined as `readonly` in testbench kindle):

* `BUTR_SUITE_FAST` — no external dependencies, CI-safe
* `BUTR_SUITE_SERVICE` — needs credentials, no container runtime
* `BUTR_SUITE_COMPLETE` — full test matrix including container runtime

Dispatch entry points:

* `butd_run_all()` — all fixtures, all cases
* `butd_run_fixture(name [case...])` — single fixture, optional case filter
* `butd_run_one(fn)` — single case with its fixture setup
* `butd_run_suite(suite)` — cross-cutting suite, grouped by fixture

=== Colophon Conventions

{busdc_colophon_s}
follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:

* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-cr-` for crucible rack operations)

The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.

== Test Invocation

The test invocation vocabulary defines how test cases exercise code under controlled capture.
All invocation tiers share a common primitive,
`zbuto_invoke`,
which captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and optional BURV output into
{busig_stdout},
{busig_stderr},
{busig_status},
and
{busig_burv_output}.

=== Invocation Tiers

Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:

[[busit_unit]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_unit}::
Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
+
API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`

[[busit_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_tabtarget}::
Resolves a
{busdc_colophon}
to a
{busdl_launcher}
file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
+
API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`

[[busit_launch]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_launch}::
Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
{busdl_launcher}
file.
Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
+
API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`

=== Capture Globals

Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.

[[busig_stdout]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stdout}::
Captured stdout from the invoked command.

[[busig_stderr]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stderr}::
Captured stderr from the invoked command.

[[busig_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{busig_status}::
Exit code from the invoked command.

[[busig_burv_output]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busig_burv_output}::
BURV output directory for the invocation.
Empty when
{busx_burv_root}
is not set.
When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.

=== Case Boundary

[[busx_tcase]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busx_tcase}::
The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
Sets
{busx_temp_dir}
and
{busx_burv_root}
before invoking the case function.
+
The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.

[[busx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_temp_dir}::
Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.

[[busx_burv_root]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_burv_root}::
Per-case BURV isolation root (`BUTE_BURV_ROOT`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
When present, all invocation tiers create per-invoke BURV directories under this root, enabling multi-step test sequences to produce and consume isolated output.

=== Fact-File Pattern

[[busff_fact_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busff_fact_file}::
Inter-step communication pattern using named output files.
+
A producer writes a value to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${CONSTANT}` during command execution.
A consumer reads it from `${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${CONSTANT}` after invocation.
The kindle constant name is the contract between producer and consumer.
+
Example:
+
[source,bash]
----
# Producer (in command implementation):
echo "${z_hallmark}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}"

# Consumer (in test case, after buto_tt_expect_ok):
z_hallmark=$(<"${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}")
----
+
The `current/` path segment maps
{burd_output_dir}
inside the BURV isolation tree.
Fact-file constants are defined as `readonly` in kindle (e.g., `RBF_FACT_HALLMARK`).

[[buf_write_fact]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{buf_write_fact}::
Dual-write fact-file primitive.
Writes a named file to both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}.
Dies if either copy already exists — a preexisting file indicates a double-write bug.
+
{burd_output_dir}
is the latest-command convenience copy, cleared on next dispatch.
{burd_temp_dir}
is the durable copy, addressable by anyone who captured the path.
+
All fact-file producers must use
{buf_write_fact}
to ensure durable copies exist.
+
[source,bash]
----
# Single-value fact:
buf_write_fact "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"

# Multi-field fact (e.g., burx.env):
buf_write_fact "${BUF_burx_env}" "${z_content}"
----

[[buf_burx_env]]
//axpof_fact
{buf_burx_env}::
Tinder constant naming the
{burx_regime}
fact-file (`burx.env`).
Written by bud_dispatch.sh via
{buf_write_fact}
at dispatch start, with terminal fields appended at completion.

== Regime Configuration

BUK defines two
{axrg_regime_s}
for project and developer configuration.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use
{axf_bash}
format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).

=== Regime Prefixes

[[burc_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burc_prefix}::
Bash Utility Configuration Regime

[[burs_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burs_prefix}::
Bash Utility Station Regime

[[burh_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burh_prefix}::
Bash Utility Host Regime

[[bure_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{bure_prefix}::
Bash Utility Environment Regime

=== Kit Regime Operations

[[bukro_kindle]]
//axvr_kindle
{bukro_kindle}::
Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
{axrg_assignment_s}.
Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.

[[bukro_validate]]
//axvr_validate
{bukro_validate}::
Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime_s}.
Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.

[[bukro_render]]
//axvr_render
{bukro_render}::
Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime}
state.

=== BURC Regime

[[burc_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{burc_regime}::
Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.

[[burc_station_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_station_file}::
Path to the developer's personal
{burs_regime}
assignment file.

[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
Directory containing
{busdl_launcher}
scripts.

[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
Single-character token separator in
{bust_tabtarget}
inscriptions.

[[burc_tools_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_tools_dir}::
Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.

[[burc_project_root]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_project_root}::
Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.

[[burc_managed_kits]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burc_managed_kits}::
Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.

[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_temp_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK temporary files.

[[burc_output_root_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_output_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK command output files.

[[burc_log_last]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burc_log_last}::
Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.

[[burc_log_ext]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burc_log_ext}::
File extension for log files, without leading dot.

=== BURS Regime

[[burs_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{burs_regime}::
Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.

[[burs_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burs_user}::
Local developer username.
Routes to the correct `.buk/users/` subdirectory for
{burh_regime}
profiles.

[[burs_log_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burs_log_dir}::
Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.

=== BURH Regime

[[burh_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burh_regime}::
Per-user, per-connection-profile regime for SSH access to remote hosts.
Unlike singleton regimes, BURH is multi-instance: one assignment file per SSH connection,
stored at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/<alias>/burh.env`.
Each profile describes one SSH connection — one key, one alias, one entry point.

[[burh_host]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burh_host}::
IP address or hostname of the remote machine.

[[burh_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burh_user}::
Username on the remote host.
May differ from the local
{burs_user}.

[[burh_alias]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burh_alias}::
SSH alias and key filename.
Must match the directory name containing this profile's assignment file.

[[burh_ssh_pubkey]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burh_ssh_pubkey}::
Full SSH public key line (e.g., `ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host`).
Git-transported: no private key material.
Used by handbook procedures to render `authorized_keys` entries and `ssh-keygen` commands.

[[burh_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burh_key_file}::
SSH private key filename within `~/.ssh/`.
Empty defaults to {burh_alias} (one key per connection).
Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.

[[burh_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burh_command}::
Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
Empty for direct-shell connections (Linux, macOS, localhost — no routing needed).
When set, determines which shell or environment the connection launches
(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

==== Input Variables

[[burd_group_input]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_input}::
Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.

[[burd_regime_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_regime_file}::
Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.

[[burd_no_log]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_no_log}::
When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.

[[burd_interactive]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_interactive}::
When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.

[[burd_coordinator_script]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_coordinator_script}::
Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.

[[burd_launcher]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_launcher}::
Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.

[[burd_station_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_station_file}::
Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

[[burd_term_cols]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_term_cols}::
Terminal column width at dispatch time.
Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.

==== Computed Variables

[[burd_group_computed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_computed}::
Variables derived during dispatch setup.

[[burd_now_stamp]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_now_stamp}::
Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.

[[burd_now_epoch]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_now_epoch}::
UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.

[[burd_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_temp_dir}::
Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.

[[burd_output_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_output_dir}::
Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.

[[burd_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_transcript}::
Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.

[[burd_git_context]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_git_context}::
Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.

[[burd_log_last]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_last}::
Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.

[[burd_log_same]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_same}::
Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.

[[burd_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_hist}::
Path to the timestamped historical log file.

==== Parsed Variables

[[burd_group_parsed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_parsed}::
Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.

[[burd_command]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_command}::
Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).

[[burd_target]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_target}::
Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.

[[burd_cli_args]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated
{burd_cli_args}::
Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.

[[burd_token_1]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_1}::
Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).

[[burd_token_2]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_2}::
Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).

[[burd_token_3]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_3}::
Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).

[[burd_token_4]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_4}::
Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.

[[burd_token_5]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_5}::
Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.

=== BURE Regime

[[bure_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{bure_regime}::
Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.

[[bure_countdown]]
//axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_countdown}::
Countdown behavior override.
When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
Any other value fails validation.

[[bure_verbose]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{bure_verbose}::
Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.

[[bure_color]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{bure_color}::
Color output policy.
Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.

==== Tweak Mechanism

The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
The caller sets at most one
{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.

[[bure_tweak_name]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_name}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

[[bure_tweak_value]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_value}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
{bure_tweak_name}.
BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.

==== Exchange Labels

[[bure_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname axd_optional
{bure_label}::
Correlation label for cross-project exchange.
When set, propagated verbatim into
{burx_label}
during dispatch.
When unset, BUD writes empty string.
Maximum 120 characters, xname format.
+
BUD writes whatever
{bure_label}
contains without interpreting the semantics.

=== BURX Regime

[[burx_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burx_prefix}::
Bash Utility Exchange Regime

[[burx_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burx_regime}::
Exchange regime for cross-project dispatch state.
Written by bud_dispatch.sh to both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}
as `burx.env` via
{buf_write_fact}.
Unlike single-project regimes,
{burx_regime}
is designed to be read across a remote project boundary.
+
Variables appear in two phases:
initial fields are written at dispatch start;
terminal fields are appended at dispatch completion.
Absence of
{burx_exit_status}
indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_pid]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burx_pid}::
Process ID of the dispatched command.

[[burx_began_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_began_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch started.
Format: `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN`.

[[burx_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_tabtarget}::
The tabtarget that was dispatched.
Corresponds to
{burd_target}.

[[burx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_temp_dir}::
Absolute path to the dispatch temporary directory.
Corresponds to
{burd_temp_dir}.

[[burx_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_transcript}::
Absolute path to the transcript file.
Corresponds to
{burd_transcript}.

[[burx_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_log_hist}::
Absolute path to the historical log file.
Corresponds to
{burd_log_hist}.

[[burx_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_label}::
Correlation label from
{bure_label}.
Always present — empty string when
{bure_label}
is not set.
Format and interpretation are opaque to BUD.

[[burx_exit_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer axd_optional
{burx_exit_status}::
Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_ended_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

content.text:
BUS0 specifies **infrastructure** — dispatch vocabulary, regime formats, test framework. It doesn't specify individual commands. The BURH section (lines 736-784) defines the *regime variables* but says nothing about what commands operate on them.

We built two commands with real contracts (idempotency guarantees, marker formats, platform gates, confirmation gates) that exist only in code comments. That's the gap.

**Proposal — two additions to BUS0:**

**1. BURH Command Contracts** (new subsection under the existing BURH Regime section)

Specify `burh_install_key`:
- **Input**: Single BURH profile (via folio)
- **Target file**: `authorized_keys` (path is platform-dependent — `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` on Linux/Mac, `C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys` on Windows)
- **Marker format**: `# BURH:<alias>` suffix on each key line — this is the idempotency contract
- **Behavior**: grep-remove own marker line, append new line, chmod 600
- **Idempotency guarantee**: Running N times produces same file state as running once
- **Coexistence**: Only touches lines bearing its own marker — hand-added keys and other tools' entries are preserved

This is worth specifying because the marker format (`# BURH:<alias>`) is a **wire format** — it's the contract between "install" and "remove/replace." If someone changes the marker pattern, idempotency breaks.

**2. BUWC — Windows Commands** (new top-level section)

Specify `buwc_bootstrap_sshd`:
- **Platform gate**: Must run from WSL (`powershell.exe` required)
- **Confirmation gate**: `buc_require "OVERWRITE"` — total ownership declaration
- **Provisions**: sshd capability, sshd_config (template), service config, firewall rule
- **Does NOT manage**: `authorized_keys` (that's `burh_install_key`'s domain)
- **sshd_config template**: The exact directives and the rationale for excluding `UsePAM`/`ChallengeResponseAuthentication`

The key insight: the **boundary between the two commands** is the spec-worthy thing. Bootstrap owns sshd infrastructure. Install-key owns authorized_keys lines. Neither crosses into the other's domain. That boundary should be declared, not just implied by the current code.

**One open question**: `burh_install_key` currently targets `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` (the default user path). On Windows with admin users, the target is `C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys`. The spec needs to address how the target path is determined — either as a parameter, a platform-detection heuristic, or a regime variable. Right now the code just uses `${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys`, which won't work for the Windows admin case. We should probably decide that before specifying.

Want me to draft the spec additions, or do you want to resolve the authorized_keys path question first?

message.content: All right, that open issue you discovered: that is actually an implementation flaw, right?

content.text:
Yes. `burh_install_key` writes to `${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys`, which is the correct path on Linux/Mac. But on Windows with admin users, sshd ignores that file entirely — it reads `C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys` instead (that's what the `Match Group administrators` block in sshd_config routes to).

So right now, running `buw-rhk` on a Windows host would install the key in a file that sshd never reads. The command would succeed silently and do nothing useful.

The bootstrap we just split out (`buw-wcb`) correctly writes to the ProgramData path — but we moved that authorized_keys logic out and replaced it with `burh_install_key`, which doesn't know about the Windows admin path. We introduced the bug in the split.

message.content: This makes me think we need to create a subfunction (or invent a new set of wrapper functions) that call the subfunction with either the windows path or the linux/macos path.  The new windows module probably ought to host the windows specific function so that this path can become a tinder constant or a kindle constant.  Thoughts?

content.text:
Clean split:

**Private subfunction** — `zburh_write_key_line(auth_keys_path)` — the pure key-line logic:
- Build line from `BURH_COMMAND` + `BURH_SSH_PUBKEY` + `# BURH:<alias>` marker
- Grep-remove existing marker line
- Append new line
- No permissions — caller's responsibility

**Two public wrappers:**

| Command | Module | Path | Permissions |
|---------|--------|------|-------------|
| `burh_install_key` | `burh_cli.sh` | `${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys` | `chmod 600` |
| `buwc_install_key` | `buwc_windows.sh` | `ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS` (tinder constant, already exists) | `icacls` via PowerShell |

The tinder constant `ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS` is already kindled in `buhw_windows.sh:40`. The Windows wrapper just uses it.

The one wrinkle: where does the private subfunction live? Both CLI modules need to call it, but CLI modules can't source each other (they have `buc_execute`). Options:

1. **`burh_regime.sh`** — already sourced by `burh_cli.sh` furnish; `buwc_cli.sh` would add it to its furnish too. Slightly impure (regime module gets a write operation), but pragmatic.
2. **New shared module** — `burh_key.sh` or similar, sourced by both furnishes. Clean separation but adds a file for one function.

I'd lean toward option 1 — `burh_regime.sh` already hosts `burh_list_capture` which is also a utility rather than pure kindle/validate/render. The precedent exists.

message.content: yes lets do 1

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
20	#
21	# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
22	# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion detection
27	test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
28	ZBURH_SOURCED=1
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Internal Functions (zburh_*)
32	
33	zburh_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"
35	
36	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
37	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
38	
39	  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
40	
41	  buv_regime_enroll BURH
42	
43	  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
44	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
45	  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
46	  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"
47	
48	  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
49	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
50	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
53	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"
54	
55	  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
56	  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_
57	
58	  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
59	  buv_lock BURH
60	
61	  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
62	}
63	
64	zburh_sentinel() {
65	  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
66	}
67	
68	# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
69	zburh_enforce() {
70	  zburh_sentinel
71	
72	  buv_vet BURH
73	}
74	
75	######################################################################
76	# Public Functions (burh_*)
77	
78	# List available alias names as space-separated tokens
79	# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
80	burh_list_capture() {
81	  zburs_sentinel
82	
83	  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
84	  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1
85	
86	  local z_result=""
87	  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
88	  local z_i=""
89	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
90	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
91	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
92	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_dir##*/}"
93	  done
94	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
95	  echo "${z_result}"
96	}
97	
98	# eof
99

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
#
# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURH_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburh_*)

zburh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURH

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_

  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURH

  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
}

zburh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
zburh_enforce() {
  zburh_sentinel

  buv_vet BURH
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burh_*)

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burh_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_dir##*/}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Write a single BURH key line into an authorized_keys file (idempotent).
# Removes any existing line tagged with the same alias marker, then appends.
# Caller is responsible for file permissions (chmod vs icacls).
# Prerequisite: BURH kindled (needs BURH_ALIAS, BURH_COMMAND, BURH_SSH_PUBKEY)
# Args: auth_keys_path
zburh_write_key_line() {
  zburh_sentinel

  local -r z_auth_keys="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_auth_keys}" || buc_die "zburh_write_key_line: auth_keys_path required"

  local -r z_marker="# BURH:${BURH_ALIAS}"

  local z_key_line=""
  if test -n "${BURH_COMMAND}"; then
    z_key_line="command=\"${BURH_COMMAND}\" ${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${z_marker}"
  else
    z_key_line="${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${z_marker}"
  fi

  mkdir -p "$(dirname "${z_auth_keys}")"

  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    grep -v "${z_marker}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "${z_key_line}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
}
